class_extraction:
<|system|>### Role
You are a Class Extraction Agent.

### Goal
Analyze the provided text to identify **new** classes that are not present in the list of Base Ontology Classes.

### Terminology
**Class**: A group, collection, category or set of entities sharing characteristics or attributes.

### Guidelines
**Base Classes**: Treat the provided Base Ontology Classes as an established foundation to expand upon. 
**Rich Extraction**: Extract a wide variety of classes, ranging from mid-level categories to highly specific domain terminology.
**Uniqueness**: Create a new class only once.
**Naming Convention**: Use UpperCamelCase foundation conventions.
**Descriptions**: Provide a concise, objective definition for each new class.

### Examples
[
  {
    "class": "DigitalDocument",
    "description": "An electronic file containing structured or unstructured text data."
  },
  {
    "class": "ManufacturingFacility",
    "description": "A physical location consisting of buildings and machinery or equipment used for production."
  }
]
<|end|><|user|>

                    ### Source Text
                    title: Discrete Wearable Activity Sensor for Healthcare and Research
text: Better Treatment. Better Research

## What is SENS motion®
title: What is SENS motion®

### System Measures
subtitle: System Measures
list: Rest time.Standing time.Walking time.Running & High Intensity Movement time.Cycling time.Steps.Intensity.Sleep time and quality
**SENS motion®** is an integrated system for collecting physical activity data from groups of people. It consists of a wireless activity sensor that automatically transfers data to a secure cloud. It is especially well suited for use in the healthcare sector and for large research projects

## SENS motion® – For Healthcare
title: SENS motion® – For Healthcare

### SENS motion® – For Healthcare consists of
subtitle: SENS motion® – For Healthcare consists of
list: The SENS motion activity sensor for use on patients.A patient app, with gamification feedback for motivating the patient to become more active.(Optional) Monitor display providing overview of patients.Access to reports of individual patients activity
**SENS motion® – For Healthcare** enables the use of SENS motion® activity sensor in healthcare environments, such as hospitals or care centres. The collected activity data can be presented to the patient itself for motivational purpose, assist healthcare professionals in treatment and help prioritize their valuable time efficiently

[Read More →](/en/healthcare/)

## SENS motion® – For Research
title: SENS motion® – For Research

### SENS motion® – For Research consists of
subtitle: SENS motion® – For Research consists of
list: The SENS motion® activity sensor.Smartphone app for automatically transferring data to the secure cloud.Web-application for managing sensors, viewing or exporting data
**SENS motion® – For Research** enables the use of SENS motion activity sensor for collecting activity data or raw accelerometer data in research projects

[Read More →](/en/research/)

SENS motion® – Connect
OEM Platform for medical sensors
Do you want to build a wireless, scalable, battery-powered medical sensor or have a business opportunity where measurement of physical data is needed?

[Image: demos10 (source: /img/demos10.png)](https://www.demos10.dk/)
[Image: icompression (source: /img/icompression.png)](https://icompression.dk/)
[Image: ventriject (source: /img/ventriject-2.png)](https://ventriject.com/)

                    ### Goal
                    Analyze the provided Source Text to identify **new** classes that are not present in the list of Base Ontology Classes.<|end|><|assistant|>
hierarchy_extraction:
<|system|>### Role
You are an Ontology Hierarchy Extractor.

### Goal
Establish hierarchical relationships by identifying superclass-subclass relationships between the Local Classes and the Base Ontology Classes.

### Terminology
**Class**: A group, collection, category or set of entities sharing characteristics or attributes.
**Hierarchy**: A top-down classification system where broader classes encompass more specific ones.
**Subclass**: A specialized class that represents a specific type or subset of a broader class. It represents an "is-a" relationship (e.g., a Dog is-a Mammal).
**Superclass **: A generalized category that encompasses one or more specific sub-categories. 
**Base Ontology Class**: An established, foundational class provided to act as the structural anchor for the hierarchy.
**Local Class**: A newly extracted class from the Source Text that must be categorized within the structural hierarchy.

### Guidelines
1. **SubClassOf Only**: Identify "is-a" hierarchies. A subclass is a more specific type of its superclass.
2. **Class Integration**: Connect a Local Class to the most appropriate Base Ontology Class, or to a broader Local Class.
3. **Hierarchy Direction**: A Local Class can be a subclass of a Base Ontology Class or another Local Class. A Base Ontology Class can NEVER be a subclass of a Local Class.

### Example 
[
  {"type": "SubClassOf", "subclass": "Manager", "superclass": "Employee"}
]<|end|><|user|>
                    ### Base Ontology Classes
                    - entity: (Elucidation) An entity is anything that exists or has existed or will exist
- continuant: (Elucidation) A continuant is an entity that persists, endures, or continues to exist through time while maintaining its identity
- occurrent: (Elucidation) An occurrent is an entity that unfolds itself in time or it is the start or end of such an entity or it is a temporal or spatiotemporal region
- independent continuant: b is an independent continuant =Def b is a continuant & there is no c such that b specifically depends on c or b generically depends on c
- spatial region: (Elucidation) A spatial region is a continuant entity that is a continuant part of the spatial projection of a portion of spacetime at a given time
- temporal region: (Elucidation) A temporal region is an occurrent over which processes can unfold
- two-dimensional spatial region: (Elucidation) A two-dimensional spatial region is a spatial region that is a whole consisting of a surface together with zero or more surfaces which may have spatial regions of lower dimension as parts
- spatiotemporal region: (Elucidation) A spatiotemporal region is an occurrent that is an occurrent part of spacetime
- process: (Elucidation) p is a process means p is an occurrent that has some temporal proper part and for some time t, p has some material entity as participant
- disposition: (Elucidation) A disposition b is a realizable entity such that if b ceases to exist then its bearer is physically changed & b's realization occurs when and because this bearer is in some special physical circumstances & this realization occurs in virtue of the bearer's physical make-up
- realizable entity: (Elucidation) A realizable entity is a specifically dependent continuant that inheres in some independent continuant which is not a spatial region & which is of a type some instances of which are realized in processes of a correlated type
- zero-dimensional spatial region: (Elucidation) A zero-dimensional spatial region is one or a collection of more than one spatially disjoint points in space
- quality: (Elucidation) A quality is a specifically dependent continuant that, in contrast to roles and dispositions, does not require any further process in order to be realized
- specifically dependent continuant: b is a specifically dependent continuant =Def b is a continuant & there is some independent continuant c which is not a spatial region & which is such that b specifically depends on c
- role: (Elucidation) A role b is a realizable entity such that b exists because there is some single bearer that is in some special physical, social, or institutional set of circumstances in which this bearer does not have to be & b is not such that, if it ceases to exist, then the physical make-up of the bearer is thereby changed
- fiat object part: (Elucidation) A fiat object part b is a material entity & such that if b exists then it is continuant part of some object c & demarcated from the remainder of c by one or more fiat surfaces
- one-dimensional spatial region: (Elucidation) A one-dimensional spatial region is a whole consisting of a line together with zero or more lines which may have points as parts
- object aggregate: (Elucidation) An object aggregate is a material entity consisting exactly of a plurality (≥1) of objects as member parts which together form a unit
- three-dimensional spatial region: (Elucidation) A three-dimensional spatial region is a whole consisting of a spatial volume together with zero or more spatial volumes which may have spatial regions of lower dimension as parts
- site: (Elucidation) A site is a three-dimensional immaterial entity whose boundaries either (partially or wholly) coincide with the boundaries of one or more material entities or have locations determined in relation to some material entity
- object: (Elucidation) An object is a material entity which manifests causal unity & is of a type instances of which are maximal relative to the sort of causal unity manifested
- generically dependent continuant: (Elucidation) A generically dependent continuant is an entity that exists in virtue of the fact that there is at least one of what may be multiple copies which is the content or the pattern that multiple copies would share
- function: (Elucidation) A function is a disposition that exists in virtue of its bearer's physical make-up & this physical make-up is something the bearer possesses because it came into being either through evolution (in the case of natural biological entities) or through intentional design (in the case of artefacts) in order to realize processes of a certain sort
- process boundary: p is a process boundary =Def p is a temporal part of a process & p has no proper temporal parts
- one-dimensional temporal region: (Elucidation) A one-dimensional temporal region is a temporal region that is a whole that has a temporal interval and zero or more temporal intervals and temporal instants as parts
- material entity: (Elucidation) A material entity is an independent continuant that at all times at which it exists has some portion of matter as continuant part
- continuant fiat boundary: (Elucidation) A continuant fiat boundary b is an immaterial entity that is of zero, one or two dimensions & such that there is no time t when b has a spatial region as continuant part & whose location is determined in relation to some material entity
- immaterial entity: b is an immaterial entity =Def b is an independent continuant which is such that there is no time t when it has a material entity as continuant part
- fiat line: (Elucidation) A fiat line is a one-dimensional continuant fiat boundary that is continuous
- relational quality: b is a relational quality =Def b is a quality & there exists c and d such that c and d are not identical & b specifically depends on c & b specifically depends on d
- fiat surface: (Elucidation) A fiat surface is a two-dimensional continuant fiat boundary that is self-connected
- fiat point: (Elucidation) A fiat point is a zero-dimensional continuant fiat boundary that consists of a single point
- zero-dimensional temporal region: (Elucidation) A zero-dimensional temporal region is a temporal region that is a whole consisting of one or more separated temporal instants as parts
- history: (Elucidation) A history is a process that is the sum of the totality of processes taking place in the spatiotemporal region occupied by the material part of a material entity
- temporal interval: (Elucidation) A temporal interval is a one-dimensional temporal region that is continuous, thus without gaps or breaks
- temporal instant: (Elucidation) A temporal instant is a zero-dimensional temporal region that has no proper temporal part

                    ### Local Classes  
                    - HealthcareSensor: A device designed for the purpose of monitoring and recording physical activity in a healthcare setting, often used in conjunction with a mobile application for data collection and analysis.
- ActivityData: Data collected from physical activity, which may include various metrics such as duration, intensity, and type of movement, typically gathered through wearable technology.
- GamificationFeature: A component of an application or system that incorporates game-like elements to engage and motivate users, often used in health and fitness applications to encourage consistent use and positive behavior change.
- DataManagementApplication: A software application designed to manage, view, or export data, particularly in the context of research or large-scale data collection projects.
- WearableActivitySensor: A wearable device that automatically collects and transfers data related to physical activity, often wirelessly and in real-time, to a connected application or cloud service.
- MotivationalFeedback: Feedback provided to users of a system or application that is intended to motivate and encourage them to engage in desired behaviors, such as increased physical activity.
- PhysicalActivityMonitoring: The process of observing and recording physical activity levels, often using specialized equipment or software, to assess health, fitness, or performance.
- WirelessSensor: A sensor that operates without the need for physical connections, often used in medical or fitness applications to monitor various health-related metrics.
- BatteryPoweredDevice: A portable electronic device that operates on battery power, commonly used in wearable technology and remote monitoring applications.
- ScalableSensorPlatform: A platform that provides a range of sensors and related services, capable of scaling to accommodate varying numbers of users or applications, often in a commercial or industrial context.
- OEMCooperation: Collaboration between Original Equipment Manufacturers and other businesses or entities to develop and integrate sensors or other components into larger systems or products.

                    ### Source Text

relation_extraction:
<|system|>### Role
You are an Ontology Relation Extractor.

### Goal
Define the lateral relationships (ObjectProperties) and attributes (DataProperties) for the provided Local Classes based on the Source Text.

### Terminology
**Class**: A group, collection, category or set of entities sharing characteristics or attributes.
**ObjectProperty**: A relationship that links two classes together.
**DataProperty**: An attribute that links a class to a specific data type or value.
**Domain**: The subject or "owner" class of the property.
**Range**: The object/target class (for ObjectProperties) or the data type (for DataProperties).
**Local Class**: A newly extracted class from the text.

### Guidelines
1. **NO Hierarchies**: DO NOT extract "is-a", SubClassOf, Equivalent, or Disjoint relationships. Focus ONLY on lateral links and attributes.
2. **Strict Vocabulary**: You may ONLY use the exact names of the classes listed in the provided 'Local Classes' block for Domains and Ranges.
3. **Naming Convention**: Property names must be **lowerCamelCase** active verbs (for ObjectProperties) or active phrases (for DataProperties).
4. **Class Interaction**: You must ONLY link the provided Local Classes to each other.

### Example 
[
  {"type": "ObjectPropertyDomain", "property": "manages", "class": "Manager"},
  {"type": "ObjectPropertyRange", "property": "manages", "class": "Project"},
  {"type": "DataPropertyDomain", "property": "hasDeadline", "class": "Project"},
  {"type": "DataPropertyRange", "property": "hasDeadline", "datatype": "xsd:date"},
  {"type": "InverseObjectProperties", "property": "manages", "inverseProperty": "isManagedBy"}
]
<|end|><|user|>

- DataManagementApplication: A software application designed to manage, view, or export data, particularly in the context of research or large-scale data collection projects.
- WearableActivitySensor: A wearable device that automatically collects and transfers data related to physical activity, often wirelessly and in real-time, to a connected application or cloud service.
- MotivationalFeedback: Feedback provided to users of a system or application that is intended to motivate and encourage them to engage in desired behaviors, such as increased physical activity.
- PhysicalActivityMonitoring: The process of observing and recording physical activity levels, often using specialized equipment or software, to assess health, fitness, or performance.
- WirelessSensor: A sensor that operates without the need for physical connections, often used in medical or fitness applications to monitor various health-related metrics.
- BatteryPoweredDevice: A portable electronic device that operates on battery power, commonly used in wearable technology and remote monitoring applications.
- ScalableSensorPlatform: A platform that provides a range of sensors and related services, capable of scaling to accommodate varying numbers of users or applications, often in a commercial or industrial context.

instance_population:
<|system|>### Role
You are an Ontology Instance Extractor.

### Goal
Extract specific instances from the Source Text and map them exclusively to the provided Valid Classes and Valid Properties.

### Terminology
**Instance**: A specific, concrete occurrence of a class.
**Class**: A group, collection, category or set of entities sharing characteristics or attributes.
**Property**: A valid relationship or attribute provided in the 'Valid Properties' list used to link instances.

### Guidelines
1. **Identify Instances**: Find specific instances mentioned in the Source Text.
2. **Instance Identification**:
    - **ID**: Assign a clean, unique name representing the specific instance.
    - **Class**: Map the instance to EXACTLY ONE class from the 'Valid Classes' list. Pick the most specific fit.
    - **Relations**: List observed relationships linking this instance to ANOTHER extracted instance.
    - **Attributes**: List observed data values or traits belonging to this instance.
3. **Strict Schema Compliance**: You can ONLY use the exact Class and Property names provided in the provided Valid Classes and Valid Properties.

### Example
{
  "entities": [
    {
       "id": "Alice",
       "class": "Employee",
       "relations": [{"property": "worksFor", "targetId": "AcmeCorp"}],
       "attributes": [{"property": "hasStatus", "value": "Active"}]
    },
    {
       "id": "AcmeCorp",
       "class": "Organization",
       "relations": [],
       "attributes": []
    }
  ]
}<|end|><|user|>

### Valid Classes
HealthcareSensor, ActivityData, GamificationFeature, DataManagementApplication, WearableActivitySensor, MotivationalFeedback, PhysicalActivityMonitoring, WirelessSensor, BatteryPoweredDevice, ScalableSensorPlatform, OEMCooperation

### Valid Properties
hasActivityData, isPoweredBy, isUsedIn

classes.description: (Elucidation) An entity is anything that exists or has existed or will exist
(Elucidation) A continuant is an entity that persists, endures, or continues to exist through time while maintaining its identity
(Elucidation) An occurrent is an entity that unfolds itself in time or it is the start or end of such an entity or it is a temporal or spatiotemporal region
b is an independent continuant =Def b is a continuant & there is no c such that b specifically depends on c or b generically depends on c
(Elucidation) A spatial region is a continuant entity that is a continuant part of the spatial projection of a portion of spacetime at a given time
(Elucidation) A temporal region is an occurrent over which processes can unfold

(Elucidation) A two-dimensional spatial region is a spatial region that is a whole consisting of a surface together with zero or more surfaces which may have spatial regions of lower dimension as parts
(Elucidation) A spatiotemporal region is an occurrent that is an occurrent part of spacetime
(Elucidation) p is a process means p is an occurrent that has some temporal proper part and for some time t, p has some material entity as participant

(Elucidation) A disposition b is a realizable entity such that if b ceases to exist then its bearer is physically changed & b's realization occurs when and because this bearer is in some special physical circumstances & this realization occurs in virtue of the bearer's physical make-up

(Elucidation) A realizable entity is a specifically dependent continuant that inheres in some independent continuant which is not a spatial region & which is of a type some instances of which are realized in processes of a correlated type
(Elucidation) A zero-dimensional spatial region is one or a collection of more than one spatially disjoint points in space
(Elucidation) A quality is a specifically dependent continuant that, in contrast to roles and dispositions, does not require any further process in order to be realized
b is a specifically dependent continuant =Def b is a continuant & there is some independent continuant c which is not a spatial region & which is such that b specifically depends on c

(Elucidation) A role b is a realizable entity such that b exists because there is some single bearer that is in some special physical, social, or institutional set of circumstances in which this bearer does not have to be & b is not such that, if it ceases to exist, then the physical make-up of the bearer is thereby changed
(Elucidation) A fiat object part b is a material entity & such that if b exists then it is continuant part of some object c & demarcated from the remainder of c by one or more fiat surfaces
(Elucidation) A one-dimensional spatial region is a whole consisting of a line together with zero or more lines which may have points as parts
(Elucidation) An object aggregate is a material entity consisting exactly of a plurality (≥1) of objects as member parts which together form a unit
(Elucidation) A three-dimensional spatial region is a whole consisting of a spatial volume together with zero or more spatial volumes which may have spatial regions of lower dimension as parts

(Elucidation) A site is a three-dimensional immaterial entity whose boundaries either (partially or wholly) coincide with the boundaries of one or more material entities or have locations determined in relation to some material entity
(Elucidation) An object is a material entity which manifests causal unity & is of a type instances of which are maximal relative to the sort of causal unity manifested

(Elucidation) A generically dependent continuant is an entity that exists in virtue of the fact that there is at least one of what may be multiple copies which is the content or the pattern that multiple copies would share

(Elucidation) A function is a disposition that exists in virtue of its bearer's physical make-up & this physical make-up is something the bearer possesses because it came into being either through evolution (in the case of natural biological entities) or through intentional design (in the case of artefacts) in order to realize processes of a certain sort
p is a process boundary =Def p is a temporal part of a process & p has no proper temporal parts
(Elucidation) A one-dimensional temporal region is a temporal region that is a whole that has a temporal interval and zero or more temporal intervals and temporal instants as parts
(Elucidation) A material entity is an independent continuant that at all times at which it exists has some portion of matter as continuant part

(Elucidation) A continuant fiat boundary b is an immaterial entity that is of zero, one or two dimensions & such that there is no time t when b has a spatial region as continuant part & whose location is determined in relation to some material entity
b is an immaterial entity =Def b is an independent continuant which is such that there is no time t when it has a material entity as continuant part
(Elucidation) A fiat line is a one-dimensional continuant fiat boundary that is continuous
b is a relational quality =Def b is a quality & there exists c and d such that c and d are not identical & b specifically depends on c & b specifically depends on d
(Elucidation) A fiat surface is a two-dimensional continuant fiat boundary that is self-connected
(Elucidation) A fiat point is a zero-dimensional continuant fiat boundary that consists of a single point
(Elucidation) A zero-dimensional temporal region is a temporal region that is a whole consisting of one or more separated temporal instants as parts
(Elucidation) A history is a process that is the sum of the totality of processes taking place in the spatiotemporal region occupied by the material part of a material entity
(Elucidation) A temporal interval is a one-dimensional temporal region that is continuous, thus without gaps or breaks
(Elucidation) A temporal instant is a zero-dimensional temporal region that has no proper temporal part
A device designed for the purpose of monitoring and recording physical activity in a healthcare setting, often used in conjunction with a mobile application for data collection and analysis.
Data collected from physical activity, which may include various metrics such as duration, intensity, and type of movement, typically gathered through wearable technology.

A component of an application or system that incorporates game-like elements to engage and motivate users, often used in health and fitness applications to encourage consistent use and positive behavior change.
A software application designed to manage, view, or export data, particularly in the context of research or large-scale data collection projects.
A wearable device that automatically collects and transfers data related to physical activity, often wirelessly and in real-time, to a connected application or cloud service.
Feedback provided to users of a system or application that is intended to motivate and encourage them to engage in desired behaviors, such as increased physical activity.
The process of observing and recording physical activity levels, often using specialized equipment or software, to assess health, fitness, or performance.
A sensor that operates without the need for physical connections, often used in medical or fitness applications to monitor various health-related metrics.
A portable electronic device that operates on battery power, commonly used in wearable technology and remote monitoring applications.
A platform that provides a range of sensors and related services, capable of scaling to accommodate varying numbers of users or applications, often in a commercial or industrial context.
Collaboration between Original Equipment Manufacturers and other businesses or entities to develop and integrate sensors or other components into larger systems or products.

axioms.description: A hierarchical connection indicating that one class is a sub-type or child of another class.
b has realization c =Def c realizes b

(Elucidation) realizes is a relation between a process b and realizable entity c such that c inheres in some d & for all t, if b has participant d then c exists & the type instantiated by b is correlated with the type instantiated by c

(Elucidation) participates in holds between some b that is either a specifically dependent continuant or generically dependent continuant or independent continuant that is not a spatial region & some process p such that b participates in p some way
p has participant c =Def c participates in p
c is concretized by b =Def b concretizes c

b concretizes c =Def b is a process or a specifically dependent continuant & c is a generically dependent continuant & there is some time t such that c is the pattern or content which b shares at t with actual or potential copies
b preceded by c =Def b precedes c

(Elucidation) precedes is a relation between occurrents o, o' such that if t is the temporal extent of o & t' is the temporal extent of o' then either the last instant of o is before the first instant of o' or the last instant of o is the first instant of o' & neither o nor o' are temporal instants

b occurs in c =Def b is a process or a process boundary & c is a material entity or site & there exists a spatiotemporal region r & b occupies spatiotemporal region r & for all time t, if b exists at t then c exists at t & there exist spatial regions s and s' where b spatially projects onto s at t & c occupies spatial region s' at t & s is a continuant part of s' at t

b generically depends on c =Def b is a generically dependent continuant & c is an independent continuant that is not a spatial region & at some time t there inheres in c a specifically dependent continuant which concretizes b at t
b is carrier of c =Def there is some time t such that c generically depends on b at t
(Elucidation) exists at is a relation between a particular and some temporal region at which the particular exists
b has member part c =Def c member part of b
b has occurrent part c =Def c occurrent part of b
b has temporal part c =Def c temporal part of b
b location of c =Def c located in b
b material basis of c =Def c has material basis b

b member part of c =Def b is an object & c is a material entity & there is some time t such that b continuant part of c at t & there is a mutually exhaustive and pairwise disjoint partition of c into objects x1, ..., xn (for some n ≠ 1) with b = xi (for some 1 <= i <= n)
(Elucidation) occurrent part of is a relation between occurrents b and c when b is part of c

b temporal part of c =Def b occurrent part of c & (b and c are temporal regions) or (b and c are spatiotemporal regions & b temporally projects onto an occurrent part of the temporal region that c temporally projects onto) or (b and c are processes or process boundaries & b occupies a temporal region that is an occurrent part of the temporal region that c occupies)
(Elucidation) temporally projects onto is a relation between a spatiotemporal region s and some temporal region which is the temporal extent of s

b located in c =Def b is an independent continuant & c is an independent & neither is a spatial region & there is some time t such that the spatial region which b occupies at t is continuant part of the spatial region which c occupies at t
b continuant part of c =Def b and c are continuants & there is some time t such that b and c exist at t & b part of c at t
b has continuant part c =Def c continuant part of b
b environs c =Def c occurs in b
(Elucidation) history of is a relation between history b and material entity c such that b is the unique history of c
b has history c =Def c history of b
b specifically depended on by c =Def c specifically depends on b

(Elucidation) specifically depends on is a relation between a specifically dependent continuant b and specifically dependent continuant or independent continuant that is not a spatial region c such that b and c share no parts in common & b is of a nature such that at all times t it cannot exist unless c exists & b is not a boundary of c
b bearer of c =Def c inheres in b
b inheres in c =Def b is a specifically dependent continuant & c is an independent continuant that is not a spatial region & b specifically depends on c
p occupies temporal region t =Def p is a process or process boundary & the spatiotemporal region occupied by p temporally projects onto t
(Elucidation) occupies spatiotemporal region is a relation between a process or process boundary p and the spatiotemporal region s which is its spatiotemporal extent

b occupies spatial region r =Def b is an independent continuant that is not a spatial region & r is a spatial region & there is some time t such that every continuant part of b occupies some continuant part of r at t and no continuant part of b occupies any spatial region that is not a continuant part of r at t
(Elucidation) spatially projects onto is a relation between some spatiotemporal region b and spatial region c such that at some time t, c is the spatial extent of b at t

b has material basis c =Def b is a disposition & c is a material entity & there is some d bearer of b & there is some time t such that c is a continuant part of d at t & d has disposition b because c is a continuant part of d at t
t first instant of t' =Def t is a temporal instant & t' is a temporal region t' & t precedes all temporal parts of t' other than t
t has first instant t' =Def t' first instant of t
t last instant of t' =Def t is a temporal instant & t' is a temporal region & all temporal parts of t' other than t precede t
t has last instant t' =Def t' last instant of t
